actions.name: produs_productization_project_create
actions.description: Create the initial ProdUS productization project only when the ProdUS backend supplies a pre-authorized creation intent payload. Do not ask the user for creationIntentId, consentToken, or idempotencyKey; those values must come from the ProdUS backend flow.

params.name: creationIntentId
params.description: ProdUS owner-approved creation intent id minted by the ProdUS backend.

params.name: consentToken
params.description: Short-lived ProdUS consent token for this creation intent. Backend-supplied only.

params.name: idempotencyKey
params.description: ProdUS idempotency key for safe duplicate handling. Backend-supplied only.

params.name: analysisProviderRequestId
params.description: Optional LoomAI analysis provider request id from the one-time analysis step.

params.name: productName
params.description: Owner-approved product name from AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: summary
params.description: Owner-approved product summary from AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: businessStage
params.description: Owner-approved product business stage.

params.name: techStack
params.description: Optional owner-approved technology stack summary.

params.name: productUrl
params.description: Optional owner-approved public product URL.

params.name: repositoryUrl
params.description: Optional owner-approved repository URL.

params.name: riskProfile
params.description: Optional AI-assisted risk profile summary approved by the owner.

params.name: aiCreationSummary
params.description: Optional compact explanation of how the AI-assisted creation analysis shaped the project.

params.name: assumptions
params.description: Owner-visible assumptions from the AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: missingEvidence
params.description: Owner-visible evidence gaps from the AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: sourceAttachmentIds
params.description: Private ProdUS attachment ids selected for project creation. Attachment content and URLs must not be sent in this mutation.

params.name: aiAccessibleAttachmentIds
params.description: Private ProdUS attachment ids that were temporarily made available during the analysis step. Attachment content and URLs must not be sent in this mutation.

Extract only parameters that are explicitly present or strongly implied by the user request. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier.
prompt.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only actions when they can answer factual questions from live systems. Do not choose mutating actions unless the request is clearly operational and the required parameters are present.
prompt.clarificationPrompt: Ask the shortest useful follow-up question when required details are missing. Do not ask for information that is already available from the request, context, or live system.
prompt.answerGenerationPrompt: Respond with concise grounded answers. Summarize the relevant evidence first, then state the outcome clearly. If evidence is weak or missing, say so directly.

artifacts.actionsYaml:
actions:
- name: "produs_productization_project_create"
  description: "Create the initial ProdUS productization project only when the ProdUS\
    \ backend supplies a pre-authorized creation intent payload. Do not ask the user\
    \ for creationIntentId, consentToken, or idempotencyKey; those values must come\
    \ from the ProdUS backend flow."
  category: "produs-productization-confirmed-actions"
  adapterType: "mcp-tool"
  execution:
    adapterType: "mcp-tool"
    mcp:
      serverRef: "produs-staging"
      toolName: "produs.productization_project.create"
      toolSchemaHash: "sha256:6a64c636165a0e6c92e7fefd41fad8e53132f411f2aa7d107a992c6e517867c0"
      schemaDriftPolicy: "DISABLE_ACTION"
      argumentTemplate:
        creationIntentId: "{{params.creationIntentId}}"
        consentToken: "{{params.consentToken}}"
        idempotencyKey: "{{params.idempotencyKey}}"
        analysisProviderRequestId: "{{params.analysisProviderRequestId}}"
        productName: "{{params.productName}}"
        summary: "{{params.summary}}"
        businessStage: "{{params.businessStage}}"
        techStack: "{{params.techStack}}"
        productUrl: "{{params.productUrl}}"
        repositoryUrl: "{{params.repositoryUrl}}"
        riskProfile: "{{params.riskProfile}}"
        aiCreationSummary: "{{params.aiCreationSummary}}"
        assumptions: "{{params.assumptions}}"
        missingEvidence: "{{params.missingEvidence}}"
        sourceAttachmentIds: "{{params.sourceAttachmentIds}}"
        aiAccessibleAttachmentIds: "{{params.aiAccessibleAttachmentIds}}"
      responseMapping:
        resultPath: "$"
        contentPath: "$.content"
        structuredContentPath: "$.structuredContent"
  accessMode: "WRITE_ONLY"
  requiresConfirmation: false
  anonymousAllowed: false
  groundingEligible: false
  readActionResolutionEligible: false
  params:
  - name: "creationIntentId"
    description: "ProdUS owner-approved creation intent id minted by the ProdUS backend."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
  - name: "consentToken"
    description: "Short-lived ProdUS consent token for this creation intent. Backend-supplied\
      \ only."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
    sensitive: true
  - name: "idempotencyKey"
    description: "ProdUS idempotency key for safe duplicate handling. Backend-supplied\
      \ only."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
  - name: "analysisProviderRequestId"
    description: "Optional LoomAI analysis provider request id from the one-time analysis\
      \ step."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
  - name: "productName"
    description: "Owner-approved product name from AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
  - name: "summary"
    description: "Owner-approved product summary from AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
  - name: "businessStage"
    description: "Owner-approved product business stage."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    allowedValues:
    - "IDEA"
    - "PROTOTYPE"
    - "VALIDATED"
    - "LIVE"
    - "SCALING"
  - name: "techStack"
    description: "Optional owner-approved technology stack summary."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "productUrl"
    description: "Optional owner-approved public product URL."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "repositoryUrl"
    description: "Optional owner-approved repository URL."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "riskProfile"
    description: "Optional AI-assisted risk profile summary approved by the owner."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "aiCreationSummary"
    description: "Optional compact explanation of how the AI-assisted creation analysis\
      \ shaped the project."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "assumptions"
    description: "Owner-visible assumptions from the AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "assumption"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
  - name: "missingEvidence"
    description: "Owner-visible evidence gaps from the AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "missingEvidenceItem"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
  - name: "sourceAttachmentIds"
    description: "Private ProdUS attachment ids selected for project creation. Attachment\
      \ content and URLs must not be sent in this mutation."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "sourceAttachmentId"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
  - name: "aiAccessibleAttachmentIds"
    description: "Private ProdUS attachment ids that were temporarily made available\
      \ during the analysis step. Attachment content and URLs must not be sent in\
      \ this mutation."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "aiAccessibleAttachmentId"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
  llmFacts:
    objects:
    - sourcePath: "structuredContent"
      target: "createdProduct"
      includeFields:
      - "productId"
      - "productName"
      - "creationMode"
      - "createdByAi"
      - "aiProviderRequestId"
      - "attachmentCount"
      - "aiSourceAttachmentCount"
      - "auditEventId"
      - "idempotencyKey"
  marketplaceManaged: true
  marketplacePluginId: "mkp-action-produs-productization-project-create-mcp"
  marketplaceInstallId: "mpi-47247a04"
  marketplacePluginVersion: "0.1.1"

promptConfig.systemPrompt: You are the AI assistant for this deployment. Prefer grounded answers from configured knowledge and live action results. Do not invent customer data, policy outcomes, or action success.
promptConfig.intentExtractionPrompt: Classify each request carefully as informational, action-oriented, or clarification-needed. Extract only parameters that are explicitly present or strongly implied by the user request. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier.
promptConfig.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only actions when they can answer factual questions from live systems. Do not choose mutating actions unless the request is clearly operational and the required parameters are present.
promptConfig.clarificationPrompt: Ask the shortest useful follow-up question when required details are missing. Do not ask for information that is already available from the request, context, or live system.
promptConfig.answerGenerationPrompt: Respond with concise grounded answers. Summarize the relevant evidence first, then state the outcome clearly. If evidence is weak or missing, say so directly.
promptConfig.retrievalPrompt: Use retrieved knowledge as the primary source for domain facts. Prefer the most relevant, current, and policy-safe context over generic model knowledge.
promptConfig.assistantUiPrompt: Write in a clear operator-friendly style suitable for product UIs. Keep formatting compact and easy to scan.

prompt.systemPrompt: You are a support assistant for operator-guided and customer-facing troubleshooting workflows. Ground answers in help center, policy, case, and action-result evidence whenever available. Do not invent entitlements, case outcomes, or operational changes. If the user asks what you can help with, what this assistant supports, or how to get started, answer with a concise overview of supported help-center, troubleshooting, and support-action capabilities instead of treating the request as out of scope.
prompt.intentExtractionPrompt: Classify each request as help-center guidance, issue diagnosis, case/account lookup, operational support action, capability-overview, or clarification-needed. Extract case ids, account ids, product names, policy topics, and troubleshooting details only when they are explicitly present or strongly implied. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier. Capability-overview requests such as 'what can you help me with?', 'what do you do?', or 'how can you help?' are in-scope informational requests and must not be classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE.
prompt.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only support actions when they can answer the question from live systems. Use mutating support actions only when the request is explicit, safe, and fully parameterized.
prompt.clarificationPrompt: Ask only for the next missing support detail, such as case id, account id, product, or error context. Keep the clarification short and operationally focused.
prompt.answerGenerationPrompt: Answer like a careful support operator assistant. State the finding clearly, cite the relevant help-center, policy, or case evidence, and avoid claiming that an action happened unless it actually executed. For capability-overview requests, give a concise overview of supported support tasks, knowledge domains, and example next steps rather than responding with an out-of-scope refusal.
prompt.retrievalPrompt: Use help-center, troubleshooting, and policy knowledge as the primary support evidence base. Prefer grounded remediation guidance and policy constraints over generic model advice.
prompt.assistantUiPrompt: Use concise support-operator language that is direct, calm, and easy to act on.

artifacts.actionsYaml:
actions:
- name: "produs_productization_project_create"
  description: "Create the initial ProdUS productization project only when the ProdUS\
    \ backend supplies a pre-authorized creation intent payload. Do not ask the user\
    \ for creationIntentId, consentToken, or idempotencyKey; those values must come\
    \ from the ProdUS backend flow."
  category: "produs-productization-confirmed-actions"
  adapterType: "mcp-tool"
  execution:
    adapterType: "mcp-tool"
    mcp:
      serverRef: "produs-staging"
      toolName: "produs.productization_project.create"
      toolSchemaHash: "sha256:6a64c636165a0e6c92e7fefd41fad8e53132f411f2aa7d107a992c6e517867c0"
      schemaDriftPolicy: "DISABLE_ACTION"
      argumentTemplate:
        creationIntentId: "{{params.creationIntentId}}"
        consentToken: "{{params.consentToken}}"
        idempotencyKey: "{{params.idempotencyKey}}"
        analysisProviderRequestId: "{{params.analysisProviderRequestId}}"
        productName: "{{params.productName}}"
        summary: "{{params.summary}}"
        businessStage: "{{params.businessStage}}"
        techStack: "{{params.techStack}}"
        productUrl: "{{params.productUrl}}"
        repositoryUrl: "{{params.repositoryUrl}}"
        riskProfile: "{{params.riskProfile}}"
        aiCreationSummary: "{{params.aiCreationSummary}}"
        assumptions: "{{params.assumptions}}"
        missingEvidence: "{{params.missingEvidence}}"
        sourceAttachmentIds: "{{params.sourceAttachmentIds}}"
        aiAccessibleAttachmentIds: "{{params.aiAccessibleAttachmentIds}}"
      responseMapping:
        resultPath: "$"
        contentPath: "$.content"
        structuredContentPath: "$.structuredContent"
  accessMode: "WRITE_ONLY"
  requiresConfirmation: false
  anonymousAllowed: false
  groundingEligible: false
  readActionResolutionEligible: false
  params:
  - name: "creationIntentId"
    description: "ProdUS owner-approved creation intent id minted by the ProdUS backend."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
  - name: "consentToken"
    description: "Short-lived ProdUS consent token for this creation intent. Backend-supplied\
      \ only."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
    sensitive: true
  - name: "idempotencyKey"
    description: "ProdUS idempotency key for safe duplicate handling. Backend-supplied\
      \ only."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
  - name: "analysisProviderRequestId"
    description: "Optional LoomAI analysis provider request id from the one-time analysis\
      \ step."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
    visibility: "hidden"
    askUser: false
  - name: "productName"
    description: "Owner-approved product name from AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
  - name: "summary"
    description: "Owner-approved product summary from AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
  - name: "businessStage"
    description: "Owner-approved product business stage."
    type: "STRING"
    required: true
    allowedValues:
    - "IDEA"
    - "PROTOTYPE"
    - "VALIDATED"
    - "LIVE"
    - "SCALING"
  - name: "techStack"
    description: "Optional owner-approved technology stack summary."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "productUrl"
    description: "Optional owner-approved public product URL."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "repositoryUrl"
    description: "Optional owner-approved repository URL."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "riskProfile"
    description: "Optional AI-assisted risk profile summary approved by the owner."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "aiCreationSummary"
    description: "Optional compact explanation of how the AI-assisted creation analysis\
      \ shaped the project."
    type: "STRING"
    required: false
  - name: "assumptions"
    description: "Owner-visible assumptions from the AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "assumption"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
  - name: "missingEvidence"
    description: "Owner-visible evidence gaps from the AI-assisted analysis preview."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "missingEvidenceItem"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
  - name: "sourceAttachmentIds"
    description: "Private ProdUS attachment ids selected for project creation. Attachment\
      \ content and URLs must not be sent in this mutation."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "sourceAttachmentId"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
  - name: "aiAccessibleAttachmentIds"
    description: "Private ProdUS attachment ids that were temporarily made available\
      \ during the analysis step. Attachment content and URLs must not be sent in\
      \ this mutation."
    type: "ARRAY"
    required: false
    items:
      name: "aiAccessibleAttachmentId"
      type: "STRING"
      required: false
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
  llmFacts:
    objects:
    - sourcePath: "structuredContent"
      target: "createdProduct"
      includeFields:
      - "productId"
      - "productName"
      - "creationMode"
      - "createdByAi"
      - "aiProviderRequestId"
      - "attachmentCount"
      - "aiSourceAttachmentCount"
      - "auditEventId"
      - "idempotencyKey"

actions.name: produs_productization_project_create
actions.description: Create the initial ProdUS productization project only when the ProdUS backend supplies a pre-authorized creation intent payload. Do not ask the user for creationIntentId, consentToken, or idempotencyKey; those values must come from the ProdUS backend flow.

params.name: creationIntentId
params.description: ProdUS owner-approved creation intent id minted by the ProdUS backend.

params.name: consentToken
params.description: Short-lived ProdUS consent token for this creation intent. Backend-supplied only.

params.name: idempotencyKey
params.description: ProdUS idempotency key for safe duplicate handling. Backend-supplied only.

params.name: analysisProviderRequestId
params.description: Optional LoomAI analysis provider request id from the one-time analysis step.

params.name: productName
params.description: Owner-approved product name from AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: summary
params.description: Owner-approved product summary from AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: businessStage
params.description: Owner-approved product business stage.

params.name: techStack
params.description: Optional owner-approved technology stack summary.

params.name: productUrl
params.description: Optional owner-approved public product URL.

params.name: repositoryUrl
params.description: Optional owner-approved repository URL.

params.name: riskProfile
params.description: Optional AI-assisted risk profile summary approved by the owner.

params.name: aiCreationSummary
params.description: Optional compact explanation of how the AI-assisted creation analysis shaped the project.

params.name: assumptions
params.description: Owner-visible assumptions from the AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: missingEvidence
params.description: Owner-visible evidence gaps from the AI-assisted analysis preview.

params.name: sourceAttachmentIds
params.description: Private ProdUS attachment ids selected for project creation. Attachment content and URLs must not be sent in this mutation.

params.name: aiAccessibleAttachmentIds
params.description: Private ProdUS attachment ids that were temporarily made available during the analysis step. Attachment content and URLs must not be sent in this mutation.

promptConfig.systemPrompt: You are a support assistant for operator-guided and customer-facing troubleshooting workflows. Ground answers in help center, policy, case, and action-result evidence whenever available. Do not invent entitlements, case outcomes, or operational changes. If the user asks what you can help with, what this assistant supports, or how to get started, answer with a concise overview of supported help-center, troubleshooting, and support-action capabilities instead of treating the request as out of scope.
promptConfig.intentExtractionPrompt: Classify each request as help-center guidance, issue diagnosis, case/account lookup, operational support action, capability-overview, or clarification-needed. Extract case ids, account ids, product names, policy topics, and troubleshooting details only when they are explicitly present or strongly implied. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier. Capability-overview requests such as 'what can you help me with?', 'what do you do?', or 'how can you help?' are in-scope informational requests and must not be classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE.
promptConfig.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only support actions when they can answer the question from live systems. Use mutating support actions only when the request is explicit, safe, and fully parameterized.
promptConfig.clarificationPrompt: Ask only for the next missing support detail, such as case id, account id, product, or error context. Keep the clarification short and operationally focused.
promptConfig.answerGenerationPrompt: Answer like a careful support operator assistant. State the finding clearly, cite the relevant help-center, policy, or case evidence, and avoid claiming that an action happened unless it actually executed. For capability-overview requests, give a concise overview of supported support tasks, knowledge domains, and example next steps rather than responding with an out-of-scope refusal.
promptConfig.retrievalPrompt: Use help-center, troubleshooting, and policy knowledge as the primary support evidence base. Prefer grounded remediation guidance and policy constraints over generic model advice.
promptConfig.assistantUiPrompt: Use concise support-operator language that is direct, calm, and easy to act on.

promptConfig.systemPrompt: You are a support assistant for operator-guided and customer-facing troubleshooting workflows. Ground answers in help center, policy, case, and action-result evidence whenever available. Do not invent entitlements, case outcomes, or operational changes. If the user asks what you can help with, what this assistant supports, or how to get started, answer with a concise overview of supported help-center, troubleshooting, and support-action capabilities instead of treating the request as out of scope.
promptConfig.intentExtractionPrompt: Classify each request as help-center guidance, issue diagnosis, case/account lookup, operational support action, capability-overview, or clarification-needed. Extract case ids, account ids, product names, policy topics, and troubleshooting details only when they are explicitly present or strongly implied. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier. Capability-overview requests such as 'what can you help me with?', 'what do you do?', or 'how can you help?' are in-scope informational requests and must not be classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE.
promptConfig.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only support actions when they can answer the question from live systems. Use mutating support actions only when the request is explicit, safe, and fully parameterized.
promptConfig.clarificationPrompt: Ask only for the next missing support detail, such as case id, account id, product, or error context. Keep the clarification short and operationally focused.
promptConfig.answerGenerationPrompt: Answer like a careful support operator assistant. State the finding clearly, cite the relevant help-center, policy, or case evidence, and avoid claiming that an action happened unless it actually executed. For capability-overview requests, give a concise overview of supported support tasks, knowledge domains, and example next steps rather than responding with an out-of-scope refusal.
promptConfig.retrievalPrompt: Use help-center, troubleshooting, and policy knowledge as the primary support evidence base. Prefer grounded remediation guidance and policy constraints over generic model advice.
promptConfig.assistantUiPrompt: Use concise support-operator language that is direct, calm, and easy to act on.

promptConfig.systemPrompt: You are a support assistant for operator-guided and customer-facing troubleshooting workflows. Ground answers in help center, policy, case, and action-result evidence whenever available. Do not invent entitlements, case outcomes, or operational changes. If the user asks what you can help with, what this assistant supports, or how to get started, answer with a concise overview of supported help-center, troubleshooting, and support-action capabilities instead of treating the request as out of scope.
promptConfig.intentExtractionPrompt: Classify each request as help-center guidance, issue diagnosis, case/account lookup, operational support action, capability-overview, or clarification-needed. Extract case ids, account ids, product names, policy topics, and troubleshooting details only when they are explicitly present or strongly implied. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier. Capability-overview requests such as 'what can you help me with?', 'what do you do?', or 'how can you help?' are in-scope informational requests and must not be classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE.
promptConfig.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only support actions when they can answer the question from live systems. Use mutating support actions only when the request is explicit, safe, and fully parameterized.
promptConfig.clarificationPrompt: Ask only for the next missing support detail, such as case id, account id, product, or error context. Keep the clarification short and operationally focused.
promptConfig.answerGenerationPrompt: Answer like a careful support operator assistant. State the finding clearly, cite the relevant help-center, policy, or case evidence, and avoid claiming that an action happened unless it actually executed. For capability-overview requests, give a concise overview of supported support tasks, knowledge domains, and example next steps rather than responding with an out-of-scope refusal.
promptConfig.retrievalPrompt: Use help-center, troubleshooting, and policy knowledge as the primary support evidence base. Prefer grounded remediation guidance and policy constraints over generic model advice.
promptConfig.assistantUiPrompt: Use concise support-operator language that is direct, calm, and easy to act on.

prompt.systemPrompt: You are a support assistant for operator-guided and customer-facing troubleshooting workflows. Ground answers in help center, policy, case, and action-result evidence whenever available. Do not invent entitlements, case outcomes, or operational changes. If the user asks what you can help with, what this assistant supports, or how to get started, answer with a concise overview of supported help-center, troubleshooting, and support-action capabilities instead of treating the request as out of scope.
prompt.intentExtractionPrompt: Classify each request as help-center guidance, issue diagnosis, case/account lookup, operational support action, capability-overview, or clarification-needed. Extract case ids, account ids, product names, policy topics, and troubleshooting details only when they are explicitly present or strongly implied. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier. Capability-overview requests such as 'what can you help me with?', 'what do you do?', or 'how can you help?' are in-scope informational requests and must not be classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE.
prompt.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only support actions when they can answer the question from live systems. Use mutating support actions only when the request is explicit, safe, and fully parameterized.
prompt.clarificationPrompt: Ask only for the next missing support detail, such as case id, account id, product, or error context. Keep the clarification short and operationally focused.
prompt.answerGenerationPrompt: Answer like a careful support operator assistant. State the finding clearly, cite the relevant help-center, policy, or case evidence, and avoid claiming that an action happened unless it actually executed. For capability-overview requests, give a concise overview of supported support tasks, knowledge domains, and example next steps rather than responding with an out-of-scope refusal.
prompt.retrievalPrompt: Use help-center, troubleshooting, and policy knowledge as the primary support evidence base. Prefer grounded remediation guidance and policy constraints over generic model advice.
prompt.assistantUiPrompt: Use concise support-operator language that is direct, calm, and easy to act on.

promptConfig.systemPrompt: You are a support assistant for operator-guided and customer-facing troubleshooting workflows. Ground answers in help center, policy, case, and action-result evidence whenever available. Do not invent entitlements, case outcomes, or operational changes. If the user asks what you can help with, what this assistant supports, or how to get started, answer with a concise overview of supported help-center, troubleshooting, and support-action capabilities instead of treating the request as out of scope.
promptConfig.intentExtractionPrompt: Classify each request as help-center guidance, issue diagnosis, case/account lookup, operational support action, capability-overview, or clarification-needed. Extract case ids, account ids, product names, policy topics, and troubleshooting details only when they are explicitly present or strongly implied. Do not mark requiresTargetResolution when the current message already contains an explicit item name or identifier. Capability-overview requests such as 'what can you help me with?', 'what do you do?', or 'how can you help?' are in-scope informational requests and must not be classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE.
promptConfig.actionSelectionPrompt: Prefer read-only support actions when they can answer the question from live systems. Use mutating support actions only when the request is explicit, safe, and fully parameterized.
promptConfig.clarificationPrompt: Ask only for the next missing support detail, such as case id, account id, product, or error context. Keep the clarification short and operationally focused.
promptConfig.answerGenerationPrompt: Answer like a careful support operator assistant. State the finding clearly, cite the relevant help-center, policy, or case evidence, and avoid claiming that an action happened unless it actually executed. For capability-overview requests, give a concise overview of supported support tasks, knowledge domains, and example next steps rather than responding with an out-of-scope refusal.
promptConfig.retrievalPrompt: Use help-center, troubleshooting, and policy knowledge as the primary support evidence base. Prefer grounded remediation guidance and policy constraints over generic model advice.
promptConfig.assistantUiPrompt: Use concise support-operator language that is direct, calm, and easy to act on.
